questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph:Consider the following sequence of reactions, if A is sulphuric acid, then give the answer of following questions.
Question:Which of the following oxy acid does not have peroxy (-O-O-) linkage?
questions.solution:
$\underset{(\mathrm{A})}{2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4} \rightarrow \underset{(\mathrm{~B})}{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_7}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \rightarrow \underset{\mathrm{D}}{\mathrm{SO}_3}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \xrightarrow{[\mathrm{O}]} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_5$
$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_6$ is hyposulphuric acid and does not have peroxy linkage. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_5$ (Caro's acid) and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_8$ (Marshall's acid) have one peroxy linkage each.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: Paragraph:A white metal sulphide $(A)$ gives $(B)$ gas with rotten egg smell and $(C)$ a colourless sulphate, when treated with dil. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$. (B) reacts with $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7 / \mathrm{H}^{+}$to form (D), a white grey element. (D) burns in oxygen to yield $(E)$, a colourless gas. If $(B)$ is added in $(E)$, it gives ( $D$ ) and a colourless liquid which turns anhydrous $\mathrm{CuSO}_4$ blue. (C) gives a precipitate with NaOH which dissolves in excess of NaOH .Question:The metal sulphide (A) is:
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph:Consider the following sequence of reactions, if A is sulphuric acid, then give the answer of following questions.
Which of the following oxy acid is having $\mathrm{S}-\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{S}$ linkage ?
questions.solution:
$\underset{(\mathrm{A})}{2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4} \rightarrow \underset{(\mathrm{~B})}{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_7}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \rightarrow \underset{\mathrm{D}}{\mathrm{SO}_3}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \xrightarrow{[\mathrm{O}]} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_5$
$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_6$ is hyposulphuric acid and does not have peroxy linkage. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_5$ (Caro's acid) and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_8$ (Marshall's acid) have one peroxy linkage each.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph:Metal nitrate $(A)$ on heating decomposes, leaving a solid residue $(B)$ which goes into solution with dilute HCl . The solution of (B) gives a white precipitate (C) with ammonium carbonate solution. The precipitate (C) is dissolved in dilute HCl and the solution is treated with potassium chromate to get yellow precipitate (D). The solution (B) with dilute $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ also gives a white precipitate $(\mathrm{E})$ insoluble in dilute HCl and nitric acid. The precipitate $(\mathrm{E})$ is a part of a white pigment lithopone.Question:The metal nitrate $(A)$ is :
questions.solution:
Lithopone is a white pigment made from a mixture of ZnS and $\mathrm{BaSO}_4$
$\underset{(\mathrm{A})}{\mathrm{Ba}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \underset{(\mathrm{~B})}{\mathrm{BaO}}+2 \mathrm{NO}_2+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{BaO}+2 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\
& \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{CO}_3 \rightarrow \mathrm{BaCO}_3 \downarrow(\mathrm{C})+2 \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl} \\
& \mathrm{BaCO}_3+2 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\mathrm{CO}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\
& \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{CrO}_4 \rightarrow \mathrm{BaCrO}_4(\mathrm{D})+2 \mathrm{KCl} \\
& \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \rightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_4+2 \mathrm{HCl}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: In oxoacids of phosphorus, P is tetrahedrally surrounded by other atoms. All these acids contain one $\mathrm{P}=$ O and $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{OH}$ bond. The oxoacids in which phosphorus has lower oxidation state (less than +5 ) contain, in addition to $\mathrm{P}=\mathrm{O}$ and $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{OH}$ bonds, either $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{P}$ (e.g., in $\mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_6$ ) or $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{H}$ (e.g., in $\left.\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_2\right)$ bonds but not both. These acids in +3 oxidation state of phosphorus tend to disproportionate to higher and lower oxidation states. For example, orthophosphorous acid (or phosphorus acid) on heating disproportionate to give orthophosphoric acid (or phosphoric acid) and phosphine. $4 \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_3 \longrightarrow 3 \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4+\mathrm{PH}_3$Question: $\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_2+\mathrm{CuSO}_4 \longrightarrow(\mathrm{X})$, a red ppt. $X$ is:
questions.solution:
Copper sulphate when reacts with hypophosphorus acid, gets reduced to cuprous hydride.
$4 \mathrm{CuSO}_4+3 \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_2+6 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{H}_2+3 \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: In oxoacids of phosphorus, P is tetrahedrally surrounded by other atoms. All these acids contain one $\mathrm{P}=$ O and $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{OH}$ bond. The oxoacids in which phosphorus has lower oxidation state (less than +5) contain, in addition to $\mathrm{P}=\mathrm{O}$ and $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{OH}$ bonds, either $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{P}$ (e.g., in $\mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_6$ ) or $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{H}$ (e.g., in $\left.\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_2\right)$ bonds but not both. These acids in +3 oxidation state of phosphorus tend to disproportionate to higher and lower oxidation states. For example, orthophosphorous acid (or phosphorus acid) on heating disproportionate to give orthophosphoric acid (or phosphoric acid) and phosphine. $4 \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_3 \longrightarrow 3 \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4+\mathrm{PH}_3$ Question: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{P}_4 \text { (white) }+\mathrm{J} \text { (an alkaline solution) } \longrightarrow \mathrm{K} \text { (reducing gas) }+\mathrm{L} \\ & \mathrm{L}+\text { dil. } \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \rightarrow \mathrm{N} \text { (ppt.) }+\mathrm{M} \text { (oxyacids } \mathrm{P} \text { ) } \end{aligned}$ N gives apple green colour in the flame. Thus J, K, L, M and N respectively are:
questions.solution:
$\underset{\text{(J)}}{3 \mathrm{Ba}(\mathrm{OH})_2}+2 \mathrm{P}_4+6 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \underset{\text{(L)}}{3 \mathrm{Ba}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{PO}_2\right)_2}+\underset{\text{(K)}}{2 \mathrm{PH}_3}$
$\underset{\text{(L)}}{\mathrm{Ba}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{PO}_2\right)_2}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \longrightarrow \underset{\text{(N)}}{\mathrm{BaSO}_4}+\underset{\text{(M)}}{2 \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_2}$
gives apple green colour in the flame
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: In oxoacids of phosphorus, P is tetrahedrally surrounded by other atoms. All these acids contain one $\mathrm{P}=$ O and $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{OH}$ bond. The oxoacids in which phosphorus has lower oxidation state (less than +5 ) contain, in addition to $\mathrm{P}=\mathrm{O}$ and $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{OH}$ bonds, either $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{P}$ (e.g., in $\mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_6$ ) or $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{H}$ (e.g., in $\left.\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_2\right)$ bonds but not both. These acids in +3 oxidation state of phosphorus tend to disproportionate to higher and lower oxidation states. For example, orthophosphorous acid (or phosphorus acid) on heating disproportionate to give orthophosphoric acid (or phosphoric acid) and phosphine. $4 \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_3 \longrightarrow 3 \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4+\mathrm{PH}_3$Question: Which of the following represents the isopolyacid of phosphorus?
questions.solution: $\mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$ (pyrophosphoric acid) is a tetrabasic acid, i.e. 4-hydroxyl groups are present. It is prepared by removing one water molecule from two molecules of orthophosphoric acid. Each phosphorus atom lies in same tetrahedral environment, so called isopolyacid.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
